GOVERNMENT REGULATION
Our businesses are subject to comprehensive U.S. federal and state and international laws and regulations. We are regulated by government agencies, which generally have discretion to issue regulations and interpret and enforce laws and regulations. U.S. federal and state and international governments continue to consider and enact various legislative and regulatory proposals which could materially impact certain aspects of the health care system and our operations. New laws and regulations, or changes in the interpretation of existing laws and regulations, including as a result of changes in the political environment, could adversely affect our businesses.
See Part I, Item 1A, "Risk Factors" for a discussion of the risks related to our compliance with U.S. federal and state and international laws and regulations.
5
Table of Contents
U.S. Federal Laws and Regulation
When we contract with the federal government, we are subject to federal laws and regulations relating to the award, administration and performance of U.S. government contracts. CMS regulates our UnitedHealthcare businesses and certain aspects of our Optum businesses. Payments by CMS to our businesses are subject to regulations, including those governing fee-for-service and the submission of information relating to the health status of enrollees for purposes of determining the amounts of certain payments to us. CMS also has the right to audit our performance to determine our compliance with CMS contracts and regulations and the quality of care we provide to Medicare beneficiaries. Our commercial business is further subject to CMS audits related to medical loss ratios (MLRs) and risk adjustment data.
UnitedHealthcare Community & State has Medicaid and CHIP contracts, which are subject to federal regulations regarding services to be provided to Medicaid enrollees, payment for those services and other aspects of these programs. There are many regulations affecting Medicare and Medicaid compliance, and the regulatory environment with respect to these programs is complex.
Our businesses are also subject to laws and regulations relating to consumer protection, anti-fraud and abuse, anti-kickbacks, false claims, prohibited referrals, inappropriate reduction or limitation of health care services, anti-money laundering and securities and antitrust compliance.
Privacy, Security and Data Standards Regulation.
Certain of our operations are subject to regulation under the administrative simplification provisions of the Health Insurance Portability and Accountability Act of 1996, as amended (HIPAA), which apply to both the group and individual health insurance markets, including self-funded employee benefit plans. Federal regulations related to HIPAA contain minimum standards for electronic transactions and code sets and for the privacy and security of protected health information.
Our businesses must comply with the Health Information Technology for Economic and Clinical Health Act (HITECH), which regulates matters relating to privacy, security and data standards. HITECH imposes requirements on uses and disclosures of health information; includes contracting requirements for HIPAA business associate agreements; extends parts of HIPAA privacy and security provisions to business associates; adds federal data breach notification requirements for covered entities and business associates and reporting requirements to HHS and the Federal Trade Commission (FTC) and, in some cases, to the local media; strengthens enforcement and imposes higher financial penalties for HIPAA violations and, in certain cases, imposes criminal penalties for individuals, including employees. In the conduct of our business, depending on the circumstances, we may act as either a covered entity or a business associate.
The use and disclosure of individually identifiable health data by our businesses are also regulated in some instances by other federal laws, including the Gramm-Leach-Bliley Act (GLBA) or state statutes implementing GLBA. These federal laws and state statutes generally require insurers to provide customers with notice regarding how their non-public personal health and financial information is used and the opportunity to "opt out" of certain disclosures before the insurer shares such information with a third party, and generally prescribe safeguards for the protection of personal information. Neither the GLBA nor HIPAA privacy regulations preempt more stringent state laws and regulations, which may apply to us, as discussed below. Federal consumer protection laws may also apply in some instances to privacy and security practices related to personally identifiable information.
ERISA.
The Employee Retirement Income Security Act of 1974, as amended (ERISA), regulates how our services are provided to or through certain types of employer-sponsored health benefit plans. ERISA is a set of laws and regulations subject to interpretation by the U.S. Department of Labor (DOL) as well as the federal courts. ERISA sets forth standards on how our business units may do business with employers who sponsor employee health benefit plans, particularly those that maintain self-funded plans. Regulations established by the DOL subject us to additional requirements for administration of benefits, claims payment and member appeals under health care plans governed by ERISA.
State Laws and Regulation
Health Care Regulation.
Our insurance and HMO subsidiaries must be licensed by the jurisdictions in which they conduct business. All of the states in which our subsidiaries offer insurance and HMO products regulate those products and operations. The states require periodic financial reports and establish minimum capital or restricted cash reserve requirements. The National Association of Insurance Commissioners (NAIC) has adopted model regulations which require expanded governance practices and risk and solvency assessment reporting. Most states have adopted these or similar measures to expand the scope of regulations relating to corporate governance and internal control activities of HMOs and insurance companies. We are required to maintain a risk management framework and file a confidential self-assessment report with state insurance regulators. We file reports annually with Connecticut, our lead regulator, and with New York, as required by the state's regulation.
Our health plans and insurance companies are regulated under state insurance holding company regulations. Such regulations generally require registration with applicable state departments of insurance and the filing of reports describing capital structure, ownership, financial condition, certain affiliated transactions and general business operations. Most state insurance

UNH.quantitative_report:
# UNH — Quantitative Report (Decision Date: 2026-04-01)

---

## 1. Revenue, Earnings, Margin, Cash-Flow, and Balance-Sheet Trajectory

**Revenue** expanded steadily: quarterly revenue rose from $99.80B (Q1-2024) to $113.22B (Q4-2025), +13.4% over eight quarters. YoY Q4-2025 revenue growth was +12.3%. TTM revenue totals $447.57B.

**Earnings** collapsed in Q4-2025. Diluted EPS fell from $6.85 → $3.74 → $2.59 → **$0.02** over the four most recent quarters, a QoQ drop of −99.2% and YoY decline of −99.7%. Net income in Q4-2025 was just $218M versus $6.47B in Q1-2025. TTM net income is $12.81B.

**Margins** reveal the source of deterioration. Gross margin is stable and high at ~88.7% ($100.53B / $113.22B in Q4-2025, comparable to ~88.8% in Q1-2024). However, operating income cratered: $9.12B in Q1-2025 → $5.15B → $4.32B → **$380M** in Q4-2025, implying an operating margin of just 0.3% in the latest quarter versus 8.3% in Q1-2025. TTM operating income is $18.96B. The margin collapse is entirely below the gross-profit line.

**Cash flow** data: unavailable for all quarters.

**Balance sheet** shows moderate growth. Total assets expanded from $284.21B (Q1-2024) to $309.58B (Q4-2025). Total equity rose from $92.37B to $100.09B. Liabilities-to-assets ratio stands at 67.2% (207.88B / 309.58B). Equity-to-assets is 32.3%.

---

## 2. Earnings Quality and Balance-Sheet Resilience

**Earnings quality is deteriorating sharply.** The Q4-2025 operating income of $380M on $113.22B of revenue implies a massive surge in operating expenses below gross profit (operating expenses of ~$100.15B versus ~$88.07B in Q1-2025), despite stable gross margins. This pattern—stable revenue and gross profit with collapsing operating income—is consistent with a large one-time charge, restructuring, or write-down, but the data do not specify the cause. The trajectory across four quarters (Q1→Q4 2025) shows a progressive deterioration, not a single-quarter spike, which raises questions about whether the charge is recurring.

**ROE** has declined from 15.5% (April 2025) to **12.8%** (latest), while net margin fell from 3.8% to **2.9%**.

**Balance-sheet resilience** is moderate. The 67.2% liability ratio is elevated but not distressed. Equity of $100.09B provides a cushion. However, with operating income collapsing, debt-service capacity is weakening rapidly on a forward-looking basis.

---

## 3. Valuation Versus Own Supplied History

| Metric | Latest (Mar-2026) | 12-Month Range | Interpretation |
|---|---|---|---|
| P/E diluted | 20.45x | 9.5x – 38.7x | Upper-mid of range, but on depressed trailing earnings |
| P/B | 2.463x | 2.2x – 5.7x | Near the bottom of its range |
| P/S | 0.551x | n/a | Low absolute multiple |
| Net margin | 2.9% | 0.0% – 10.0% | At trough of range |
| ROE | 12.8% | 10% – 20% | At lower end |

The current P/E of 20.45x sits near the middle of the observed window (9.5x–38.7x), but this is misleading: it is elevated *because* the denominator (earnings) has collapsed, not because the price is high. The stock traded at 10.8x P/E in August 2025 when EPS was running at $6+ per quarter. At the current depressed earnings run-rate (~$0.02/quarter annualized to ~$0.08), the P/E would be astronomically high on a forward-looking basis.

P/B at 2.463x is near the floor of its 2.2x–5.7x range, suggesting the market is pricing in significant impairment of earning power.

---

## 4. Price Level and Return Context Versus Fundamentals

**Current price:** $270.59 (March 31, 2026)

| Period | Price | Return |
|---|---|---|
| 52-week high | $606.36 | Stock is −55.4% below this level |
| 52-week low | $234.60 | Stock is +15.3% above this level |
| Annual return | — | **−47.6%** |
| Quarterly return (Q1-2026) | — | **−17.7%** |

**Quarterly path of returns:**
- Q1-2025 (Mar→Jun): −39.5%
- Q2-2025 (Jun→Sep): +10.6%
- Q3-2025 (Sep→Dec): −4.7%
- Q4-2025 (Dec→Mar): −17.7%

The price decline broadly tracks the earnings collapse: the steepest quarterly drop (−39.5% in Q1-2025) coincided with the beginning of the earnings deterioration. The stock stabilized temporarily in Q2-2025 when EPS was still $3.74, but has since resumed selling as earnings continued to erode. Price behavior is fundamentally consistent—not a dislocation from fundamentals, but a repricing of impaired earnings.

---

## 5. Strongest Quantitative Upside and Downside

**Upside:**
- If Q4-2025 operating income of $380M is a one-time trough and operating income normalizes toward the Q1–Q3-2025 average of ~$6.20B/quarter, annualized operating income could revert to ~$24.8B, implying TTM net income near $18–20B and EPS near $20. At a normalized P/E of 15–18x, the implied price range is $300–$360, representing **+11% to +33%** upside from $270.59. P/B would return toward the middle of its historical range (~3.5–4.0x), further supporting the case.
- Revenue growth remains robust at +12.3% YoY, providing a fundamental base if cost issues are resolved.

**Downside:**
- If the operating-income collapse is structural or driven by recurring cost increases (medical loss ratio, regulatory, or competitive pressure), Q4-2025 earnings could persist or worsen. Annualized EPS at $0.08 would make the stock at $270.59 essentially unbounded on P/E. The stock could retest the 52-week low of $234.60 (−13.3%) or break below it.
- The progressive quarterly earnings deterioration ($6.85 → $3.74 → $2.59 → $0.02) has not shown signs of reversal in the data available.
- With no cash-flow data, the ability to assess whether the company is generating sufficient cash to support operations, dividends, and balance-sheet obligations is **unavailable**.

---

## 6. Provisional Quantitative Risk/Reward Conclusion

UNH presents a **deeply uncertain risk/reward profile** at the decision date. Revenue growth remains solid (+12.3% YoY, TTM $447.57B), and the balance sheet retains $100.09B of equity. However, the Q4-2025 operating income collapse to $380M—a 95.8% decline from Q1-2025's $9.12B—represents a severe impairment of earnings power whose cause is indeterminate from the numbers alone. The stock's −47.6% annual return broadly reflects this deterioration rather than presenting a clear dislocation.

**P/B near its 12-month floor (2.463x)** and depressed valuation metrics suggest some margin of safety is priced in. Yet without cash-flow evidence, without clarity on whether the operating cost surge is one-time or structural, and with a progressive four-quarter earnings decline showing no reversal, the quantitative case for a mean-reversion trade is **unproven**. The upside is conditional on earnings normalization (~$300–$360); the downside is contingent on continued impairment toward the $234.60 low or below. The burden of proof rests on the next quarter's operating income trajectory.
UNH.qualitative_report:
**Qualitative Assessment Report: UnitedHealth Group (UNH)**
**Decision Date: 2026-04-01**

### 1. Business/Franchise Description
Based on the 2026-03-02 10-K filing, UnitedHealth Group operates two complementary, integrated businesses:
*   **Optum:** An information and technology-enabled health services business with three segments: Optum Health (patient-centered care for 95 million consumers, including value-based arrangements), Optum Insight (data, analytics, and consulting for health systems/plans), and Optum Rx (pharmacy care services managing $188B in spending in 2025).
*   **UnitedHealthcare:** A health benefits provider serving 29.7M employer/individual members, 8.4M Medicare Advantage members, and nearly 7.4M community/state members. It is a major recipient of government payments, with CMS premium revenues representing 44% of UNH's 2025 consolidated revenues.

The franchise is characterized by significant scale, vertical integration between payer (UnitedHealthcare) and service (Optum) businesses, and a focus on data analytics and value-based care models.

### 2. Recent Developments (Dated Events)
*   **2026-03-30:** UnitedHealth was added to the Schwab U.S. Dividend Equity ETF (SCHD) during its annual reconstitution, increasing the fund's healthcare allocation.
*   **2026-03-23:** Market commentary noted UNH trading at a forward P/E of 15x and described it as a "recovery story" implementing cost-cutting measures after underestimating healthcare costs.
*   **2026-03-06:** Web context indicated investments in technology (analytics, telehealth) are viewed as long-term growth catalysts.
*   **2026-03-02:** Cantor Fitzgerald described the proposed "Great Healthcare Plan" as a favorable outcome for insurers and characterized a recent UNH sell-off as overdone (Finance.yahoo.com).
*   **2026-03-01:** A broad market risk was noted, with Warren Buffett's retirement and Berkshire Hathaway's large stock sales cited as a warning amid high S&P 500 valuations.
*   **2026-02-18:** News highlighted challenges from 2025 (leadership changes, government probes, rising costs) and a new challenge from a Trump administration proposal to hold Medicare Advantage payment rates flat in 2027.
*   **2026-02-17:** UnitedHealthcare Medicare Advantage coverage was expanded for the Lifeward ReWalk Personal Exoskeleton.
*   **2026-02-11:** Web context confirmed 2025 results showed revenue growth of 12% to $447.6 billion, with adjusted EPS of $16.35 slightly ahead of expectations.

### 3. Material Qualitative Risks and Offsets
**Risks:**
*   **Regulatory & Reimbursement Pressure (Confirmed):** The proposed flat 2027 Medicare Advantage payment rate (2026-02-18) could pressure profitability for this key segment, which generates substantial government revenue.
*   **Legal & Governance Exposure (Confirmed):** The company faces active lawsuits from activist investors compelling disclosure on the impact of its vertical integration (2026-03-25 to 2026-03-27). Past cybersecurity incidents, fraud allegations, and an antitrust lawsuit are also cited (2026-02-18).
*   **Cost Trend Uncertainty (Confirmed):** Commentary notes UNH has previously "struggled with underestimated healthcare costs" (2026-03-23), indicating ongoing risk in medical cost management.
*   **Broad Market Overvaluation (Inferred Context):** The note on high S&P 500 valuations (2026-03-01) suggests a risk-laden macro environment, though not UNH-specific.

**Offsets:**
*   **Diversified, Integrated Model (Confirmed):** The business structure combining insurance with health services/data analytics is presented as a core strength for navigating system changes (10-K).
*   **Recovery & Cost Actions (Inference from Narrative):** The characterization as a "recovery story" with cost-cutting (2026-03-23) suggests management is actively addressing near-term pressures.
*   **Policy Tailwinds (Inference):** The proposed "Great Healthcare Plan" is viewed as favorable for insurers (2026-03-02), potentially offsetting some regulatory headwinds.
*   **Robust Market Position (Confirmed):** Scale across Medicare, Medicaid, and commercial markets provides a significant competitive moat, reflected in continued inclusion in major ETFs (2026-03-30).

### 4. Qualitative Assessment
**Neutral with Positive Underpinnings and Elevated Near-Term Risks.**

The business franchise is robust, well-diversified, and strategically positioned in high-growth areas like value-based care and data analytics. The confirmed 2025 financial performance was solid. However, the qualitative narrative is currently dominated by material near-term risks: specific regulatory threats to Medicare Advantage rates, active litigation challenging its corporate structure, and acknowledged historical volatility in medical cost estimation. While the integrated model provides long-term offset and there are signals of a recovery narrative, the preponderance of dated negative developments outweighs the positive catalysts in the point-in-time evidence provided.

corrections.issue: Quant report states 'ROE has declined from 15.5% (April 2025) to 12.8% (latest)' and 'net margin fell from 3.8% to 2.9%'. The raw evidence shows ROE=0.155 (15.5%) at 2025-04-01 and ROE=0.128 (12.8%) at 2026-03-31, and net_margin=0.038 (3.8%) at 2025-04-01 and 0.029 (2.9%) at 2026-03-31. These figures are consistent with the raw data. However, the quant report also states 'operating margin of just 0.3% in the latest quarter' while the raw valuation ratios show operating_margin=0.042 (4.2%) for the latest period. The 0.3% figure is derived from Q4-2025 operating income of $380M / $113.22B revenue = 0.34%, which is a quarterly calculation. The 4.2% in the valuation ratios likely reflects a TTM or different basis. The report should clarify which basis is being used to avoid confusion.
corrections.required_action: Add a clarifying note that the 0.3% operating margin is a single-quarter (Q4-2025) figure, while the 4.2% operating margin in the valuation ratios panel likely reflects a trailing or adjusted basis. This prevents the reader from seeing an apparent contradiction.

corrections.issue: The qual report states 'The confirmed 2025 financial performance was solid' citing 'revenue growth of 12% to $447.6 billion, with adjusted EPS of $16.35 slightly ahead of expectations.' However, the raw quantitative evidence shows a severe Q4-2025 earnings collapse (EPS $0.02, operating income $380M). The qual report's characterization of 'solid' financial performance is based on the web context summary of the earnings call (2026-02-11) which references adjusted EPS of $16.35 for full-year 2025. The GAAP EPS trajectory tells a very different story. The qual report does not acknowledge this tension.
corrections.required_action: The qual report should note that while adjusted/full-year metrics appeared solid per management commentary, the GAAP quarterly trajectory shows severe deterioration in H2-2025, creating ambiguity about the true earnings quality.

corrections.issue: The quant report's upside scenario assumes 'operating income normalizes toward the Q1–Q3-2025 average of ~$6.20B/quarter.' The raw data shows Q1=$9.12B, Q2=$5.15B, Q3=$4.32B, average=$6.20B. This is arithmetically correct but the trajectory was already sharply declining (Q1→Q3), so using a simple average may overstate the normalization level. The report does acknowledge this is conditional, but could note the declining trend within Q1-Q3.
corrections.required_action: Consider noting that the Q1-Q3 average is skewed by the strong Q1 figure, and the trend within those quarters was already deteriorating, which weakens the normalization case.

unsupported_claims: : The qual report's characterization of 2025 financial performance as 'solid' is sourced from a web context summary referencing adjusted EPS of $16.35, but this conflicts with the raw GAAP quarterly EPS trajectory showing collapse from $6.85 to $0.02. The report does not reconcile these two views.

: The qual report states 'CMS premium revenues representing 44% of UNH's 2025 consolidated revenues' — this is confirmed in the 10-K raw evidence ('Premium revenues from CMS represented 44% of UnitedHealth Group's total consolidated revenues for the year ended December 31, 2025'). This is supported.

: The quant report's claim that 'the stock traded at 10.8x P/E in August 2025 when EPS was running at $6+ per quarter' — the raw evidence shows pe_diluted=10.776 on 2025-08-12, which is consistent. However, the claim that 'EPS was running at $6+ per quarter' is slightly misleading: Q2-2025 EPS was $3.74 and Q3-2025 was $2.59, so by August 2025 the trailing EPS was not $6+ per quarter. The 10.8x P/E likely reflected trailing 12-month earnings that included the strong Q1-2025 ($6.85) and prior year quarters.

pm_decision_obj.executive_summary: UNH's robust revenue growth (+12.3% YoY) and durable franchise are offset by a severe, unexplained earnings collapse in Q4-2025 (operating income $380M vs. $9.12B in Q1-2025) whose cause and duration remain indeterminate. The stock's -47.6% annual decline largely reflects this impairment, leaving valuation near trough P/B (2.46x) but with insufficient visibility into earnings normalization to justify a directional call.
pm_decision_obj.investment_thesis: UNH's robust revenue growth (+12.3% YoY) and durable franchise are offset by a severe, unexplained earnings collapse in Q4-2025 (operating income $380M vs. $9.12B in Q1-2025) whose cause and duration remain indeterminate. The stock's -47.6% annual decline largely reflects this impairment, leaving valuation near trough P/B (2.46x) but with insufficient visibility into earnings normalization to justify a directional call.
key_factors: Q4-2025 operating income collapsed to $380M (0.3% margin) from $9.12B in Q1-2025, a 95.8% decline across four progressively worsening quarters ($6.85→$3.74→$2.59→$0.02 EPS), with no cash-flow data to assess underlying cash generation.

Revenue remains robust at $447.57B TTM with +12.3% YoY growth, and the integrated Optum/UnitedHealthcare franchise serves 29.7M commercial members plus 8.4M Medicare Advantage members, providing a durable fundamental base.

P/B of 2.463x is near its 12-month floor (2.2x–5.7x range), and the stock is -55.4% below its 52-week high of $606.36, suggesting significant impairment is priced in, but the progressive earnings deterioration has not yet shown reversal.

Regulatory headwinds include proposed flat 2027 Medicare Advantage payment rates (2026-02-18) and active litigation challenging vertical integration (2026-03-25 to 2026-03-27), while CMS represents 44% of consolidated revenues, amplifying reimbursement sensitivity.

Gaming
Microsoft is expanding how billions of people globally access and play video games on PC, console, mobile, and cloud. Our game content is developed through a collection of first-party studios creating iconic and differentiated gaming experiences. The gamer remains at the heart of the Xbox ecosystem. We are identifying new opportunities to attract gamers across a variety of different end points through our first- and third-party content and business diversification across subscriptions, ads, and digital stores. We've seen new devices from third-party manufacturers along with key PC and mobile end points that help us empower gamers to play in a way that is most convenient to them. We are focused on growing the platform and expanding to new ecosystems to engage as many gamers as possible.
Xbox enables people to connect and share online gaming experiences that are accessible on Xbox consoles, Windows-enabled devices, and other devices. Xbox is designed to benefit users by providing access to a network of certified applications and services and to benefit our developer and partner ecosystems by providing access to a large customer base. Xbox revenue is mainly affected by subscriptions and sales of first- and third-party content, as well as advertising. Growth of our Gaming business is determined by the overall active user base through Xbox enabled content, availability of games, providing exclusive game content that gamers seek, the computational power and reliability of the devices used to access our content and services, and the ability to create new experiences.
Search and News Advertising
Our Search and news advertising business is designed to deliver relevant search, native, and display advertising to a global audience. Devices face competition from various computer, tablet, and hardware manufacturers who offer a unique combination of high-quality industrial design and innovative technologies across various price points. ## MSFT Quantitative Report — 2026-04-01

### 1. Trajectory

**Revenue:** Consistent sequential growth from $61.86B (2024-03-31 Q3) to $81.27B (2025-12-31 Q2), a cumulative gain of ~31%. TTM revenue = $305.45B. Latest QoQ growth = +4.6%.

**Earnings:** EPS climbed from $2.94 to $5.16 over the same span. The Q2 spike is sharp: +38.7% QoQ ($3.72 → $5.16) on only +4.6% revenue growth. TTM net income = $119.26B.

**Margins:** Gross margin stable at 68.0%–70.1% (latest 68.0%). Operating margin range 43.1%–48.9% (latest 47.1%). Net margin averaged ~35–37% for the prior six quarters but spiked to **47.3%** in Q2 (38.46B / 81.27B), a clear outlier.

**Cash flow:** Unavailable for every reported quarter. FCF conversion cannot be assessed.

**Balance sheet:** Total assets $484.27B → $665.30B (+37%). Total equity $253.15B → $390.88B (+54%). Total debt **declined** from $44.91B to $40.26B. Cash = $89.46B, implying **net cash of $49.2B**.

---

### 2. Earnings Quality & Balance-Sheet Resilience

**Earnings quality — flag:**
- Net income ($38.46B) **exceeds** operating income ($38.27B) by $0.19B in Q2. Under any positive tax regime, net income should be materially below operating income. This signals a non-operating gain, tax benefit, or other non-recurring item embedded in the quarter.
- The +38.6% QoQ net-income jump versus +3.1% gross-profit growth and only +0.8% operating-income growth ($37.96B → $38.27B) further suggests the earnings beat is **not driven by core operations**.

**Balance-sheet resilience — strong:**
- Debt-to-equity = 0.119 (11.9%).
- Net cash position of $49.2B.
- Equity grew every quarter in the dataset.
- No leverage risk identified.

---

### 3. Valuation vs. Own History

| Metric | Current | 12-mo Range | Position |
|---|---|---|---|
| P/E (diluted) | **26.3** | 25.4 – 40.6 | **Near floor** |
| EV/EBIT | **19.9** | 19.2 – 32.3 | **At floor** |
| P/B | **7.61** | 7.3 – 11.9 | **Near floor** |
| P/S | **9.41** | — | — |
| Earnings yield | **3.8%** | — | — |

From the sparse history: PE compressed **34%** from 39.6 (Jul 2025) to 26.3 (Mar 2026); EV/EBIT compressed **37%** from 31.5 to 19.9 over the same window. The stock is priced at or near its cheapest levels across every supplied valuation metric.

**Critical caveat:** If the $5.16 EPS contains a non-recurring boost (as section 2 suggests), the **normalised** PE on ~$3.70–3.80 EPS would be ~30–35x, materially above the stated 26.3x. The headline cheapness may therefore be illusory.

---

### 4. Price Level & Return Context

| Period | Price Start | Price End | Return |
|---|---|---|---|
| Q1 2025 (Mar–Jun) | $378.80 | $497.41 | **+31.3%** |
| Q2 2025 (Jun–Sep) | $497.41 | $514.60 | +3.5% |
| Q3 2025 (Sep–Dec) | $514.60 | $487.10 | −5.3% |
| Q4 2025 (Dec–Mar) | $487.10 | $370.17 | **−24.0%** |
| Annual (Mar '25–Mar '26) | $378.80 | $370.17 | **−2.3%** |

52-week high = $555.45; low = $344.79. Current price is **7.4%** above the low and **33%** below the high. All of the Mar–Jun 2025 rally has been erased. The annual return is **−2.3%** against a backdrop of ~31% revenue growth and ~75% EPS growth over the period, reflecting a substantial valuation de-rating rather than fundamental deterioration (ex the Q2 quality question).

---

### 5. Strongest Quantitative Upside & Downside

**Upside:**
- If Q2 EPS of $5.16 proves broadly sustainable, TTM EPS ≈ $16.00. A re-rating to even the low-30s PE (e.g., 32x) implies a price of ~$512, or **+38%** from current. Mean-reversion to the 12-month median PE of ~36x implies ~$576 (+56%).
- Revenue trajectory is unambiguously positive and accelerating in dollar terms.

**Downside:**
- If Q2 EPS is flattered by a non-recurring item, normalised TTM EPS near $15.0–15.2 implies a "true" PE of ~28x on the stated 26.3x multiple — not cheap. Any further de-rating below the current 12-month floor of 25.4x on normalised earnings could take the stock below $344.79 (the 52-week low).
- Three consecutive negative quarters and no available cash-flow data to corroborate earnings make the path uncertain.
- Gross margin trending down (70.1% → 68.0%) could compress further.

---

### 6. Provisional Quantitative Risk/Reward Conclusion

The stock sits at or near **cycle-low valuations** across P/E (26.3), EV/EBIT (19.9), and P/B (7.61), while revenue and operating income trajectories are clearly positive and the balance sheet carries $49.2B net cash with 11.9% D/E. The price has round-tripped a year of gains despite strong fundamental progress — a valuation-driven drawdown.

However, the Q2 earnings exhibit a **material quality concern**: net income exceeding operating income and a +38.7% EPS jump on +4.6% revenue growth point to a non-recurring tailwind. On normalised EPS, the stock is likely **fairly valued** at ~30–33x rather than the headline 26x.

**Conditional assessment:** If Q2 earnings durability is confirmed by subsequent filings, the risk/reward skews positive at current depressed multiples. If Q2 proves anomalous, the stock is closer to fair value with limited margin of safety and further downside risk given the ongoing price downtrend. The absence of cash-flow data is a material blind spot that prevents a high-conviction conclusion on earnings quality.
MSFT.qualitative_report:
### **Qualitative Report: Microsoft (MSFT)**
**Decision Date: 2026-04-01**

#### **1. Business/Franchise Read from Supplied Narrative**
Microsoft's business is structured around three integrated segments focused on cloud computing and AI: Productivity and Business Processes (Microsoft 365, LinkedIn, Dynamics), Intelligent Cloud (Azure, server products), and More Personal Computing (Windows, Gaming, Search). The 10-K (2025-07-30) describes its mission as a technology platform company empowering digital transformation and AI adoption. Its competitive moat is built on enterprise entrenchment across its stack, the scale of the Microsoft Cloud, and integrated AI-driven security and productivity tools. Key growth pillars are AI monetization (e.g., Copilot, Azure AI), cloud infrastructure (Azure), and the expansion of its gaming ecosystem post-Activision acquisition.

#### **2. Recent Developments (Dated from Supplied Text)**
*   **Market Sentiment & Valuation (2026-03-31):** MSFT stock has declined 33% from its October 2025 peak. Current market commentary frames the stock at a 23x P/E ratio as potentially undervalued versus historical patterns, given its earnings growth and enterprise position.
*   **Geopolitical Catalyst (2026-03-31):** A broad tech market rally on March 31, 2026, was triggered by signals of de-escalation in the Iran conflict, improving risk sentiment for growth stocks. This rally was part of a volatile month where major indexes faced their worst performance since September 2022.
*   **AI & Cloud Catalysts (2026-02-27, 2026-03-03, 2026-03-13):** Identified forward catalysts include accelerating Azure backlog conversion, the monetization of Microsoft 365 Copilot through new pricing tiers, and the conversion of a $625 billion commercial RPO base. Azure growth was noted to have faced capacity constraints in the fiscal second quarter.
*   **Legal & Regulatory Risks (2026-02-17, 2026-03-16, 2026-03-18):** The company faces active lawsuits and regulatory probes. A class action lawsuit alleges Microsoft Teams illegally captured biometric data (2026-02-17). Microsoft is also involved in litigation related to video game addiction (2026-03-16) and is weighing a lawsuit against OpenAI and Amazon over a cloud dispute while already facing regulatory probes (2026-03-18).

#### **3. Material Qualitative Risks and Offsets**
*   **Risk: Legal & Regulatory Headwinds.** The narrative shows multiple concurrent lawsuits (biometric data, gaming addiction) and regulatory scrutiny, particularly around AI data practices and privacy. These create financial risk and could constrain product development or create public backlash.
    *   **Offset: Deep Enterprise Integration.** The 10-K emphasizes Microsoft's position as a secure, integrated platform for businesses, suggesting high switching costs and trusted relationships that may mitigate competitive threats from AI-first or cloud-only rivals.
*   **Risk: Geopolitical & Market Volatility.** Recent news highlights market sensitivity to geopolitical events (e.g., Iran conflict), which directly impacts sentiment toward growth and tech stocks like MSFT.
    *   **Offset: Diversified Business Model.** The company operates across productivity, cloud, and consumer segments. News (2026-03-31) positions it as a balanced, "smart choice" for cautious investors due to diversified revenue streams, providing some defensive characteristics.
*   **Risk: Execution on AI Strategy.** Capacity constraints impacted Azure growth, and there is narrative concern about dependence on key partners like OpenAI. Criticism exists regarding AI data utilization practices (2026-03-26).
    *   **Offset: Scale and Breadth of AI Portfolio.** The 10-K details a full-stack AI strategy, from Azure AI Foundry to Copilot integrated across all products. This breadth, combined with a massive installed base, provides multiple monetization paths and a platform advantage.

#### **4. Concise Qualitative Assessment**
**Neutral, with Positive Underlying Franchise Strength.**
The assessment is **Neutral** in the near term due to the confluence of significant legal/regulatory pressures and pronounced stock volatility linked to external market factors. However, the foundational business franchise remains **positive**. The company's entrenched position in enterprise cloud and productivity, coupled with a broad, integrated AI strategy and strong network effects, provides a durable competitive advantage. The key qualitative tension is between these strong structural offsets and the material, near-term risks from litigation and market sentiment that are currently weighing on the narrative.

pm_decision_obj.executive_summary: MSFT trades at cycle-low valuations (P/E 26.3x, EV/EBIT 19.9x) with a strong balance sheet ($49.2B net cash, 11.9% D/E) and clear revenue growth (+31% cumulative), but a material earnings quality concern in Q2 (net income exceeding operating income, +38.7% EPS jump on +4.6% revenue) means the headline cheapness is likely illusory on normalized earnings. The risk/reward is mildly positive but conviction is tempered by the absence of cash-flow data and ongoing legal/regulatory headwinds.
pm_decision_obj.investment_thesis: MSFT trades at cycle-low valuations (P/E 26.3x, EV/EBIT 19.9x) with a strong balance sheet ($49.2B net cash, 11.9% D/E) and clear revenue growth (+31% cumulative), but a material earnings quality concern in Q2 (net income exceeding operating income, +38.7% EPS jump on +4.6% revenue) means the headline cheapness is likely illusory on normalized earnings. The risk/reward is mildly positive but conviction is tempered by the absence of cash-flow data and ongoing legal/regulatory headwinds.
key_factors: Cycle-low valuations: P/E of 26.3x and EV/EBIT of 19.9x are at or near 12-month floors, with the stock 33% below its 52-week high of $555.45 despite 31% cumulative revenue growth over the same period.

Earnings quality concern: Q2 2025-12-31 net income ($38.46B) exceeds operating income ($38.27B), a pattern unique to this quarter in the dataset, suggesting a non-recurring tailwind; normalized EPS of ~$3.70-3.80 implies a true P/E of ~30-33x rather than the headline 26.3x.
Balance sheet strength: Net cash position of $49.2B with debt-to-equity of 11.9% and total equity growing every quarter, providing significant financial resilience.

AI and cloud catalysts ahead: $625B commercial RPO base, Azure backlog conversion, and Microsoft 365 Copilot monetization represent concrete forward catalysts, though Azure capacity constraints and legal risks (biometric data lawsuit, gaming addiction litigation) create execution uncertainty.

HUMAN CAPITAL RESOURCES
At NIKE, we consider the strength and effective management of our workforce to be essential to the ongoing success of our business. We believe that it is important to attract, develop and retain an engaged workforce with diverse experiences, backgrounds and perspectives at all levels of our business and that such a workforce fosters creativity and accelerates innovation.
CULTURE
Each employee shapes NIKE's culture through behaviors and practices. This starts with our Maxims, which represent our core values and, along with our Code of Conduct, feature the fundamental behaviors that help anchor, inform and guide us and apply to all employees. Our mission is to bring inspiration and innovation to every athlete in the world, which includes the belief that if you have a body, you are an athlete. We aim to do this by creating groundbreaking sport innovations, making our products more sustainably, building a creative global team with diverse experiences, backgrounds and perspectives, supporting the well-being of our employees and making a positive impact in communities where we live and work. Our mission is aligned with our deep commitment to maintaining an environment where all NIKE employees have the opportunity to reach their full potential, to connect to our brands and to shape our workplace culture. We believe providing for growth and retention of our employees is essential in fostering such a culture and are dedicated to providing access to training programs and career development opportunities, including trainings on NIKE's values, history and business, trainings on developing leadership skills at all levels, tools and resources for managers and qualified tuition reimbursement opportunities.
We are committed to having an inclusive and diverse team and culture, and accessible workplace. We achieve this through recruitment, development and retention of qualified talent with diverse experiences, backgrounds and perspectives through traditional channels, initiatives and partnerships, including those that serve colleges and universities. Additionally, we provide access to education so that all NIKE employees and leaders have the cultural knowledge and understanding to lead inclusively and build diverse and inclusive teams. We also have employee resource groups, collectively known as NikeUNITED, that promote NIKE cultural awareness and are open to all.
In empowering our employees to help shape our culture, we source employee feedback through a variety of survey tools: our annual Engagement Survey program, corporate pulse surveys and listening sessions. These tools provide employees throughout the globe an opportunity to provide confidential feedback on key areas known to drive employee engagement, including their satisfaction with their managers, their work and the Company generally. These tools also measure our employees' connection to NIKE's culture. NIKE also provides multiple points of contact for employees to speak up if they experience something that does not align with our values or otherwise violates our workplace policies, even if they are uncertain what they observed or heard is a violation of company policy.
As part of our commitment to making a positive impact on our communities, we maintain a goal of investing 2% of our prior fiscal year's pre-tax income into global communities. The focus of this investment continues to be inspiring youth to be active through play and sport as well as uniting and inspiring communities. Through our investments we bring the power of sport into our communities, with the goal of making play and sport more accessible. Our community investments are an important part of our culture, and we support employees in giving back to community organizations through volunteering and donations, which are matched by the NIKE Foundation where eligible.
EMPLOYEE BASE
As of May 31, 2025, we had approximately 77,800 employees worldwide, including retail and part-time employees. We also utilize independent contractors and temporary personnel to supplement our workforce.
Most of our employees are not represented by unions, except for certain employees in the EMEA and APLA geographies who are members of and/or are represented by trade unions, as allowed or required by local law and/or collective bargaining agreements. Also, in some countries outside of the United States, local laws require employee representation by works councils (which may be entitled to information and consultation on certain subsidiary decisions) or by organizations similar to a union. In certain European countries, we are required by local law to enter into, and/or comply with, industry-wide or national collective bargaining agreements. NIKE has never experienced a material interruption of operations due to labor disagreements.
COMPENSATION AND BENEFITS
NIKE's total rewards are intended to be competitive and equitable, meet the varied needs of our global teammates and reinforce our values. We are committed to providing comprehensive, competitive and equitable pay and benefits to our employees, and we have invested, and aim to continue to invest, in our employees through growth and development and holistic well-being initiatives. NKE.quantitative_report:
### Quantitative Report: NKE (2026-04-01)

#### 1. Revenue, Earnings, Margin, Cash-Flow, and Balance-Sheet Trajectory
- **Revenue**: Flat YoY growth of +0.6% in Q2 2025 (12.43B vs 12.35B). Sequentially, QoQ growth of +6.0% from Q1 2025 (11.72B). TTM revenue: 46.51B.
- **Earnings**: Sharp YoY decline: EPS diluted -32.1% (0.53 vs 0.78). Net income -31.9% (792M vs 1.16B). TTM net income: 2.52B.
- **Margins**: Deteriorating across all levels:
  - Gross margin: 40.5% in Q2 2025 (5.04B/12.43B), down from 43.6% YoY.
  - Operating margin: 8.0% (999M/12.43B), down from 11.5% YoY.
  - Net margin: 6.4% (792M/12.43B), down from 9.4% YoY.
- **Cash-Flow**: No data supplied for any period; unavailable for assessment.
- **Balance-Sheet Trajectory**: Total assets stable at 37.79B (Q2 2025) vs 37.96B (Q2 2024). Total equity stable at 14.09B vs 14.04B. Equity/assets ratio ~37.3%. Total debt decreased to 7.96B (Q4 2024) from 8.90B (Q4 2023).

#### 2. Earnings Quality and Balance-Sheet Resilience
- **Earnings Quality**: Margins have compressed significantly over 7 quarters: net margin from 11.9% (Q4 2023) to 6.4% (Q2 2025); operating margin from 13.7% to 8.0%. YoY EPS decline outpaces revenue decline, indicating cost pressures or mix deterioration. No cash-flow data to assess accrual quality.
- **Balance-Sheet Resilience**: Leverage is moderate with equity/assets ~37%. Debt reduced by 0.94B over 4 quarters. However, declining profitability weakens debt coverage capacity; interest coverage cannot be computed due to missing data.

#### 3. Valuation Versus Supplied History
- **Latest Valuation (2026-03-31)**: PE diluted=30.99, P/B=5.554, P/S=1.682, earnings yield=3.2%, net margin=5.4%, ROE=17.9%.
- **Historical Range (2025-04-01 to 2026-03-31)**: PE diluted ranged from 17.5 to 38.9; P/B from 5.4 to 8.9. Current PE is mid-range (30.99 vs midpoint ~28.2), while P/B is near the low end (5.554 vs low 5.4). Net margin has declined from 10.0% to 5.4%, and ROE from 34.8% to 17.9% over the same period.

#### 4. Price Level and Return Context vs Fundamentals
- **Price**: Close=52.82 (2026-03-31), near 52-week low of 50.95. Quarterly return: -13.7% (from 61.21). Annual return: -16.5% (from 63.29).
- **Fundamental Context**: YoY EPS decline (-32.1%) exceeds price decline (-16.5%), indicating valuation multiple expansion (PE from ~19.7 to 31.0). Quarterly price decline (-13.7%) contrasts with QoQ EPS growth (+8.2%) and revenue growth (+6.0%).

#### 5. Strongest Quantitative Upside and Downside
- **Upside**: P/B near historical low (5.554 vs range 5.4–8.9) suggests potential re-rating if profitability stabilizes. Sequential revenue and earnings growth in Q2 2025 (+6.0% and +8.2% QoQ) may signal improving momentum.
- **Downside**: Persistent margin compression (net margin from 10.0% to 5.4% in one year) risks further earnings deterioration. Elevated PE (30.99) with declining earnings (YoY -32.1%) increases downside risk if multiples revert toward historical lows (PE 17.5). Price near 52-week low with negative momentum (-13.7% quarterly) may continue if fundamentals disappoint.

#### 6. Provisional Quantitative Risk/Reward Conclusion
The quantitative evidence shows deteriorating earnings quality and margin pressure, with a PE still elevated relative to its own history (30.99 vs one-year low of 17.5). Price has declined more than fundamentals over the past year but remains vulnerable given the earnings trajectory. Balance-sheet resilience is moderate but untested by cash-flow data. The risk/reward is asymmetric to the downside: further margin erosion could drive price lower, while a recovery would require stabilization of profitability metrics not yet evident in the supplied numbers.
NKE.qualitative_report:
### Qualitative Assessment: NKE (Nike, Inc.) – Decision Date 2026-04-01

**1. Business/Franchise Read (from 2025 10-K, filed 2025-07-17)**
Nike is the world's largest seller of athletic footwear and apparel. Its franchise is built on designing, developing, and marketing products under the NIKE, Jordan, and Converse brands. The business operates through a hybrid distribution model: Nike Direct (owned retail stores and digital platforms) and a global wholesale network. Product strategy emphasizes innovation in performance and style. The company has a vast global supply chain, with nearly all footwear and apparel manufactured by independent contractors, primarily in Vietnam, Indonesia, and China. Its brand strength, scale, and innovation capabilities are core competitive advantages in a highly competitive industry.

**2. Recent Developments (from supplied text, 2026-02-10 to 2026-03-31)**
*   **Turnaround Execution & Financial Expectations (2026-03-31):** Nike is executing a turnaround strategy focused on rebuilding wholesale relationships, growing in running, and clearing inventory. However, investor sentiment remains wary due to persistent headwinds. Analysts expect Q3 FY2026 sales to decline ~0.4% and earnings to fall significantly (e.g., projected 45-48% drop in adjusted EPS per articles on 2026-03-31 and 2026-03-29).
*   **Regional Challenges (2026-03-31):** The turnaround faces ongoing pressure from weak demand in China and sluggish growth in North America.
*   **Cost Headwinds & Tariffs (2026-03-30, 2026-03-25):** New tariffs are a material concern, expected to cost Nike upwards of $1.5 billion annually in 2026 and reduce gross margins by ~1.2% (cited on 2026-03-25 and 2026-03-30).
*   **Potential Catalysts (2026-03-31, 2026-03-05):** The FIFA World Cup 2026, for which Nike is the primary sponsor for host nations and top teams, is highlighted as a key near-term catalyst. Some analysis suggests the company could achieve mid-single-digit revenue growth over 1-2 years if product innovation and retail restructuring succeed (2026-03-05).
*   **External Market Environment (2026-03-30, 2026-03-20):** Broader market conditions are challenging, with soaring oil prices (> $115/barrel) due to Middle East tensions contributing to inflationary pressures and market volatility. Nike's stock hit a 52-week low on 2026-03-20.

**3. Material Qualitative Risks and Offsets**
*   **Risks:**
    *   **Tariff & Trade Policy Exposure (Confirmed):** The 10-K (2025-07-17) explicitly details the risk of trade protectionism. Recent news (2026-03-30) quantifies the near-term financial impact at ~$1.5B annually.
    *   **Legal & Regulatory Exposure (Confirmed):** Ongoing lawsuits include allegations over RTFKT NFTs as unregistered securities (2026-03-16), a consumer protection lawsuit regarding email marketing (2026-03-04), and a newly filed class-action over a data breach (2026-03-31). A DEI-related EEOC investigation is also noted (2026-02-18).
    *   **Competitive Pressure & Brand Fatigue (Confirmed/Inferred):** The 10-K (2025-07-17) states the industry is "highly competitive." Recent narrative (2026-03-30) infers "brand fatigue" from over-reliance on retro models.
    *   **Macroeconomic & Demand Uncertainty (Confirmed):** Weak consumer demand, particularly in key markets like China and North America, is cited consistently in earnings previews (2026-03-31). Soaring energy costs (2026-03-30) threaten broader consumer spending.
*   **Offsets:**
    *   **Inherent Brand Equity & Scale (Confirmed):** Nike's position as the global market leader is a foundational strength per the 10-K.
    *   **Strategic Catalysts (Inferred):** The FIFA World Cup 2026 (2026-03-31) presents a significant, time-bound opportunity for marketing and sales uplift.
    *   **Turnaround Initiatives (Inferred):** Management actions on wholesale, running, and inventory (2026-03-31) are aimed at correcting past strategic missteps. Some analysis sees a foundation for recovery in FY2027 (2026-03-25).

**4. Concise Qualitative Assessment**
**Neutral.** Nike's franchise remains fundamentally strong due to its unmatched brand, scale, and innovation heritage. However, the near-term narrative is dominated by significant operational headwinds (weak demand, tariff costs, inventory issues) and a series of legal risks. The success of the current turnaround strategy is unproven, and the macroeconomic environment is hostile. While the FIFA World Cup offers a clear catalyst, the balance of confirmed challenges and speculative offsets currently tilts the qualitative picture to a neutral stance.

Valuation stretched relative to history: PE diluted of 30.99 sits well above the one-year low of 17.5x, while P/B of 5.554 is near its historical floor of 5.4, suggesting the market is pricing in a recovery that has not yet materialized in the fundamentals.

Our Shared Value Priorities
As part of our purpose to help people save money and live better, we seek to operate our business in a way that creates shared value. We believe we maximize long-term value and competitive advantage by delivering for stakeholders, customers, associates, shareholders, suppliers, partners and communities. Addressing their needs strengthens our business by building trust, creating opportunity, managing cost and risk, developing future capabilities and reinforcing the systems on which we rely.
We prioritize stakeholder issues with the greatest potential to create long-term shared value – those most relevant to our business, important to stakeholder trust and where Walmart can make a meaningful impact.
- Opportunity. Expanding economic opportunity for associates, suppliers and communities helps us attract and retain talent, meet customer needs and strengthen resilience. As described further below, our workforce strategy focuses on preparing our workforce for the future by aligning skills with evolving business needs and investing in career pathways and learning. We also support supplier growth through development programs and sourcing from a diverse mix of local and global suppliers.
- Sustainability. Walmart's sustainability efforts focus on enhancing the resilience of our operations and product value chains to enhance surety of supply, catalyze innovation and growth, maintain everyday low cost and build stakeholder trust. Our priorities include reducing greenhouse gas emissions, regenerating natural resources, reducing product and packaging waste and supporting people who work in supply chains through responsible sourcing and the creation of economic opportunity.
- Community. We serve customers globally through our omnichannel model and contribute to community vitality by providing quality jobs and training, investing in local suppliers, supporting causes important to customers and associates and assisting communities during crises and natural disasters.
- Ethics and Integrity. We foster trust by promoting ethics and compliance, maintaining strong governance and oversight, engaging responsibly in public policy, using data and technology responsibly and respecting human rights.
We report periodically on these priorities through environmental, social and governance disclosures on our corporate website, which are not incorporated by reference into this Annual Report on Form 10-K or incorporated by reference into any of our other filings with the SEC.
Human Capital Management
As Walmart grows, the way we attract, develop and reward talent – and design how work gets done – evolves alongside our business. Our business is focused on serving people and this is delivered by our approximately 2.1 million associates around the world with approximately 1.6 million associates in the U.S. and approximately 0.5 million associates internationally. In the U.S., approximately 92% of our associates are hourly and approximately 68% of our associates are full-time. Our workforce strategy reflects our commitment to creating a future-ready workforce, supporting associate growth, and fostering a culture where associates can thrive.
Workforce Strategy and Enablement.
Our workforce strategy focuses on aligning our organizational structure, talent capabilities and technology investments with the evolving needs of the business, including development of a digitally skilled, AI-enabled workforce. This includes preparing associates for new roles, technologies and ways of working, as well as deploying digital tools that support associate effectiveness, engagement and performance. As part of this effort, we are making everyday work simpler and more meaningful by reshaping roles to emphasize uniquely human strengths such as creativity and leadership and identifying areas where AI can automate repetitive tasks.
We are committed to maintaining fair and competitive workforce practices as we evolve how work gets done. We regularly review our workforce practices to support consistency, accountability and long-term sustainability, and we maintain a performance-based culture where associates are rewarded based on meaningful factors such as qualifications, experience, performance and the work they do.
10
Associate Growth and Development.
Investment in associate growth and development supports skill-building, leadership development and overall business performance. Development programs are designed to meet both individual and business needs, offering multiple career pathways and learning opportunities across roles, levels and geographies. Our development approach focuses on building leadership, technical and professional capabilities and equipping associates with the skills required for a changing environment. This includes company‑wide AI learning pathways and certifications designed to meet associates where they are.
Internal career mobility is an important component of our talent model. Approximately 75% of U.S. salaried store, club and supply chain management associates began their careers in hourly positions, reflecting long‑term advancement opportunities within the Company.
Development is further supported through targeted programs such as Walmart Academy, which provides training in retail skills, leadership and well‑being, along with Live Better U, which offers eligible associates access to high school diplomas, certificates, skills credentials and college degrees that are aligned to business needs and in-demand roles.
By investing in development, career mobility and skills, we strengthen our associate value proposition and support a workforce capable of adapting as our business evolves.
Associate Experience and Engagement.
Our efforts focus on supporting associate well-being, listening and a culture of belonging. We prioritize the financial, physical and mental well-being of our associates by offering competitive wages and a broad range of benefits designed to meet the diverse needs of our global workforce and their eligible dependents. In the U.S., these benefits include a 401(k) match, Associate Stock Purchase Plan match, associate discounts, predictable scheduling practices, paid time off, life insurance, medical coverage (for most plans, this includes no-cost virtual care and no-cost centers of excellence program for certain complex conditions), behavioral health services, family building

WMT.quantitative_report:
**Quantitative Assessment: WMT (Wal-Mart Stores Inc.)**  
*Decision Date: 2026-04-01 | Latest Filing: 2026-03-13 (Q4 2026)*

---

### 1. Trajectory: Revenue, Earnings, Margins, Cash Flow, and Balance Sheet
**Revenue & Earnings:**
- Latest Q4 revenue: **190.66B** (YoY +5.6%, QoQ +6.2%); TTM revenue: **713.16B**.
- Q4 EPS diluted: **0.53** (YoY -19.7%, QoQ -31.2%); TTM net income: **22.27B**.
- Quarterly EPS trajectory (newest first): 0.53 → 0.77 → 0.88 → 0.56 → 0.66 → 0.57 → 0.63.  
  *Note: Sharp EPS drop in Q4 despite revenue growth.*

**Margins:**
- Q4 gross margin: **24.7%** (47.04B / 190.66B); operating margin: **4.6%** (8.71B / 190.66B); net margin: **2.3%** (4.39B / 190.66B).
- Margins stable over trailing quarters but compressed vs. Q2 2025 (operating margin 6.0%).

**Cash Flow:**
- *Unavailable:* No cash flow data supplied for any period.

**Balance Sheet:**
- Latest total assets: **284.67B**, equity: **105.89B**, debt: **38.17B**.
- Debt-to-equity: **0.36** (38.17B / 105.89B). Equity grew from 88.07B (2024-04-30) to 105.89B (2026-01-31).

---

### 2. Earnings Quality & Balance-Sheet Resilience
**Earnings Quality:**
- Volatile; EPS declined from 0.88 (Q2 2025) to 0.53 (Q4 2026) despite steady revenue growth.  
- Operating income fell QoQ from 7.13B → 6.70B → 7.29B → 8.71B, suggesting variability.  
- *Cash conversion cannot be assessed due to missing cash flow data.*

**Balance-Sheet Resilience:**
- Moderate leverage (D/E 0.36).  
- Equity increased **+20.2%** over 7 quarters (88.07B → 105.89B).  
- *Note: Some balance sheet entries show negative total liabilities (e.g., 2025-07-31: -96.55B), which may indicate data inconsistency.*

---

### 3. Valuation vs. Company History
- Current PE diluted: **45.5** (range: 33.9–55.3 over past year).  
- Current P/B: **9.4** (range: 6.8–11.9).  
- Net margin: **3.1%** (range: 2.8–3.0%); ROE: **21%** (range: 20.7–21.6%).  
- Valuation multiples expanded from ~36.9 PE (April 2025) to 45.5, while margins/ROE stable.

---

### 4. Price Context vs. Fundamentals
- **Annual return:** +46.0% (from 85.15 to 124.28).  
- **Quarterly return:** +10.4% (from 112.53 to 124.28).  
- Price trades near 52-week high (134.69 vs. low 79.81).  
- *Disconnect:* Price appreciation despite Q4 EPS decline, placing stock in top half of historical valuation range.

---

### 5. Strongest Quantitative Upside & Downside
**Upside:**  
- If EPS stabilizes near TTM levels (~0.55/quarter) and PE remains elevated (mid-range ~45), stock could hold value. Revenue growth (+5.6% YoY) supports top-line momentum.  
- Balance sheet health (D/E 0.36) limits financial risk.

**Downside:**  
- Significant EPS volatility (latest -31.2% QoQ) may signal deteriorating earnings quality.  
- Decline to lower PE range (e.g., 33.9) would imply ∼25% downside from current valuation without earnings growth.

---

### 6. Provisional Risk/Reward Conclusion  
Quantitative evidence shows **elevated valuation** (PE 45.5 vs. historical 33.9–55.3) paired with **recent earnings weakness** (Q4 EPS 0.53, -19.7% YoY). Price has outperformed fundamentals (+46% annual) despite margin compression and EPS volatility. Balance sheet resilience is mitigated by missing cash flow data and inconsistent liability figures.  
**Risk-reward is unfavorable:** price appears priced for stability and growth, but recent earnings trajectory does not support this. Downside from multiple contraction or earnings miss outweighs upside unless earnings quality improves markedly.
WMT.qualitative_report:
**Qualitative Equity Research Report: Walmart (WMT)**
**Decision Date:** 2026-04-01
**Analyst:** Qualitative Analyst
**Evidence Basis:** Point-in-time evidence dated before 2026-04-01.

### 1. Business/Franchise Read (From Supplied Narrative)
Based on the latest 10-K filing (2026-03-13), Walmart is a global omnichannel retailer operating over 10,900 stores across 19 countries and serving approximately 280 million customers weekly. The business is structured into three segments:
*   **Walmart U.S. (68% of FY26 net sales):** Operates 4,611 stores under Walmart and Neighborhood Market brands, plus eCommerce. Strategy centers on "Everyday Low Price" (EDLP) and "Everyday Low Cost" (EDLC), with a strong focus on grocery and an expanding omnichannel ecosystem including pickup/delivery, Walmart+, marketplace, and advertising.
*   **Walmart International (19% of FY26 net sales):** Operates 5,743 stores across 18 countries, including key markets like Mexico, India (Flipkart/PhonePe), and Canada. Focuses on leveraging global expertise with local execution to drive profitable growth in omnichannel and related services.
*   **Sam's Club U.S. (13% of FY26 net sales):** A membership-only warehouse club with 601 clubs. Model relies on membership income and offers a curated assortment, including the Member's Mark private brand.

The overarching strategy is to build a technology-powered, customer-centric retail ecosystem that integrates physical and digital assets, supported by investments in AI, automation, and supply chain. Newer, higher-margin revenue streams in advertising, marketplace, and fulfillment services are emphasized as mutually reinforcing growth engines.

### 2. Dated Recent Developments
**Financial/Operational:**
*   **2026-02-26:** For the quarter ending Jan 2026, Walmart U.S. posted **4.6% comparable sales growth**, driven by price-led traffic, grocery demand, and omnichannel use (Morningstar analysis).
*   **2026-03-17:** Company guidance for FY2027 (ending Jan 2027) expects **constant-currency net sales growth of 3.5%-4.5%**, described as a "noticeable deceleration" from the holiday quarter's pace (The Motley Fool).

**Valuation & Investor Sentiment:**
*   **2026-03-26:** Noted that Walmart's valuation had reached **43x earnings**, prompting a **recent 6% pullback** from highs. The article contrasts this valuation unfavorably with peers like Amazon (28x) and TSMC (The Motley Fool).
*   **2026-03-28:** Walmart was recommended as a **safe stock for uncertain times** due to its pricing power, dividend king status, and expanding advertising segment (The Motley Fool).

**Legal/Regulatory:**
*   **2026-02-26 & 2026-02-27:** Walmart agreed to a **$100 million settlement** with the FTC and multiple states over allegations it misled delivery drivers about potential earnings and tipped pay (TechCrunch, Statesman.com, FTC.gov).
*   **2026-03-23:** Reference to the conclusion of a separate, unrelated "Walmart overcharging lawsuit settlement for weighted goods" (CraigWatkins Law).

### 3. Material Qualitative Risks and Offsets
**Risks:**
1.  **Legal and Regulatory:** The company faces ongoing litigation and regulatory scrutiny. The **$100M FTC settlement (2026-02-26)** is a confirmed cost. The **Estée Lauder lawsuit (2026-02-15)** tests marketplace oversight, creating reputational and operational risk. As a global operator, it is subject to complex and varying laws and regulations.
2.  **Competitive Intensity:** The 10-K explicitly states competition is "highly competitive and rapidly evolving," with threats from omnichannel, eCommerce, and new business models. Competitors may have advantages in specific areas like brand recognition, supplier terms, or agility.
3.  **Valuation and Growth Deceleration:** Recent commentary highlights a **stretched valuation (43x earnings as of 2026-03-26)** relative to some mega-cap peers. This is compounded by company guidance for **moderating sales growth (3.5%-4.5% for FY27)**, potentially limiting upside surprise.

**Offsets:**
1.  **Scale and Integrated Model:** The vast store footprint (**10,900+ locations**), logistics network, and omnichannel capabilities are cited as key competitive advantages, enabling cost leadership (EDLP/EDLC) and customer convenience.
2.  **Growth Catalysts Beyond Core Retail:** Management emphasizes high-potential growth areas like **digital advertising, marketplace, and fulfillment services**. These leverage existing assets and customers to improve margin mix. **Walmart Connect** and international operations like **Flipkart** are noted growth levers.
3.  **Resilient Demand & Trust:** The business is characterized as a **defensive asset** in uncertain economic climates due to its focus on price leadership and consumer staples, as noted in the 2026-03-28 analysis. The EDLP model builds persistent customer trust.

### 4. Concise Qualitative Assessment
**Neutral.**
The franchise, as defined in the 10-K, is structurally strong, with unrivaled scale, an evolving omnichannel model, and a clear strategy to build higher-margin ancillary businesses. This provides a durable competitive moat and defensive characteristics. However, the near-term narrative is more nuanced. Evidence points to **moderating forward growth guidance** and a **valuation that has been perceived as stretched**, contributing to recent share price pressure. Furthermore, **confirmed legal settlements** represent tangible cash costs and highlight regulatory risks. While long-term catalysts (advertising, international eCommerce) are credible, the recent developments introduce caution regarding the pace of growth and execution costs. The balance between fundamental strength and current valuation/growth dynamics warrants a neutral stance.

***Evidence Note:** All assessments are derived strictly from the provided text. No financial projections or external data have been used.*

corrections.issue: Quant report states 'Net margin: 3.1% (range: 2.8–3.0%)' — the range appears to be from the sparse valuation history where net_margin values are 0.03, 0.03, 0.03, 0.028, 0.028, 0.028, 0.028, 0.028, 0.028, 0.028, 0.028, 0.031. The range should be 2.8%–3.1%, not 2.8%–3.0%, since the latest value is 3.1%. This is a minor arithmetic error but could mislead on whether the latest margin is within or above the historical range.
corrections.required_action: Correct the net margin range to 2.8%–3.1% to reflect the full data set including the latest 3.1% reading.

corrections.issue: Quant report states 'Operating income fell QoQ from 7.13B → 6.70B → 7.29B → 8.71B, suggesting variability.' This sequence is presented as if it shows a decline, but the actual trajectory from oldest to newest (Q1 2025 through Q4 2026) is 7.13B → 7.29B → 6.70B → 8.71B. The latest Q4 operating income of 8.71B is actually the highest in the series, not evidence of deterioration. The framing is misleading.
corrections.required_action: Reframe the operating income trajectory accurately: it dipped in Q3 2025 (6.70B) but recovered strongly in Q4 2026 to 8.71B, the highest in the 7-quarter series. This is a positive signal, not merely 'variability.'

corrections.issue: Quant report notes 'Some balance sheet entries show negative total liabilities (e.g., 2025-07-31: -96.55B), which may indicate data inconsistency.' The raw data confirms this anomaly (2025-07-31 total_liabilities=-96.55B, 2025-04-30 total_liabilities=-90.34B, 2024-04-30 total_liabilities=-88.07B). However, the report flags this but then proceeds to use equity figures from those same periods without caveat. The negative liabilities likely reflect a data-feed error (equity equals total assets in those rows), which means the equity growth calculation (88.07B → 105.89B) may be overstated if the 88.07B figure is unreliable.
corrections.required_action: Acknowledge that the equity growth claim (+20.2%) depends on a potentially unreliable 2024-04-30 equity figure tied to the negative-liability data anomaly. The D/E ratio and equity trend should carry a data-quality caveat.

unsupported_claims: Quant report: 'Net margin: 3.1% (range: 2.8–3.0%)' — the latest data point is 3.1%, so the range upper bound should be 3.1%, not 3.0%. The 3.1% reading is above the stated range, which is factually incorrect.

Quant report: 'Operating income fell QoQ from 7.13B → 6.70B → 7.29B → 8.71B' — this ordering is not QoQ (newest first); the actual chronological order is 7.13B (Q1 2025) → 7.29B (Q2 2025) → 6.70B (Q3 2025) → 8.71B (Q4 2026). The latest quarter shows a strong recovery, contradicting the implied negative framing.

pm_decision_obj.executive_summary: Walmart's franchise is structurally sound with strong scale, omnichannel capabilities, and defensive demand characteristics, but the stock is trading at an elevated valuation (PE ~45x) that already prices in much of the good news. Recent earnings weakness (Q4 EPS -19.7% YoY) and moderating FY27 guidance (3.5%-4.5% sales growth) create a risk-reward that is roughly balanced, with limited upside unless earnings quality improves.
pm_decision_obj.investment_thesis: Walmart's franchise is structurally sound with strong scale, omnichannel capabilities, and defensive demand characteristics, but the stock is trading at an elevated valuation (PE ~45x) that already prices in much of the good news. Recent earnings weakness (Q4 EPS -19.7% YoY) and moderating FY27 guidance (3.5%-4.5% sales growth) create a risk-reward that is roughly balanced, with limited upside unless earnings quality improves.
key_factors: Elevated valuation: PE of 45.5x sits in the upper half of the 33.9-55.3 historical range, with the stock up 46% over the past year despite Q4 EPS declining 19.7% YoY to $0.53 — price has outpaced fundamental improvement.
Revenue momentum remains solid: Q4 revenue of $190.66B grew 5.6% YoY with Walmart U.S. comparable sales of 4.6%, supported by grocery demand, traffic growth, and expanding omnichannel adoption.

Operating income recovery: Q4 operating income of $8.71B was the highest in the 7-quarter series, recovering from a Q3 2025 dip to $6.70B, suggesting the Q4 EPS decline was driven by below-the-line items rather than core operational deterioration.

Growth deceleration and legal costs: FY2027 guidance of 3.5%-4.5% constant-currency sales growth represents a noticeable deceleration, while the $100M FTC settlement (2026-02-26) and ongoing litigation add cost and regulatory risk to the outlook.

Item 1. Business.
Overview
JPMorgan Chase & Co. ("JPMorganChase" or the "Firm", NYSE: JPM), a financial holding company incorporated under Delaware law in 1968, is a leading financial services firm based in the United States of America ("U.S."), with operations worldwide. JPMorganChase had $4.4 trillion in assets and $362.4 billion in stockholders' equity as of December 31, 2025. The Firm is a leader in investment banking, financial services for consumers and small businesses, commercial banking, financial transaction processing and asset management. Under the J.P. Morgan and Chase brands, the Firm serves millions of customers, predominantly in the U.S., and many of the world's most prominent corporate, institutional and government clients globally.
JPMorganChase's principal bank subsidiary is JPMorgan Chase Bank, National Association ("JPMorgan Chase Bank, N.A."), a national banking association with U.S. branches in 48 states and Washington, D.C. JPMorganChase's principal non-bank subsidiary is J.P. Morgan Securities LLC ("J.P. Morgan Securities"), a U.S. broker-dealer. The bank and non-bank subsidiaries of JPMorganChase operate nationally as well as through overseas branches and subsidiaries, representative offices and subsidiary foreign banks. The Firm's principal operating subsidiaries outside the U.S. are J.P. Morgan Securities plc and J.P. Morgan SE ("JPMSE"), which are subsidiaries of JPMorgan Chase Bank, N.A. and are based in the United Kingdom ("U.K.") and Germany, respectively.
The Firm's website is www.jpmorganchase.com. JPMorganChase makes available on its website, free of charge, annual reports on Form 10-K, quarterly reports on Form 10-Q and current reports on Form 8-K pursuant to Section 13(a) or Section 15(d) of the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, as soon as reasonably practicable after it electronically files or furnishes such material to the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (the "SEC") at www.sec.gov. JPMorganChase makes new and important information about the Firm available on its website at https://www.jpmorganchase.com, including on the Investor Relations section of its website at https://www.jpmorganchase.com/ir. Information on the Firm's website, including documents on the website that are referenced in this Form 10-K, is not incorporated by reference into this Annual Report on Form 10-K for the year ended December 31, 2025 ("2025 Form 10-K" or "Form 10-K") or the Firm's other filings with the SEC.
Business segments & Corporate
For management reporting purposes, the Firm has three reportable business segments – Consumer & Community Banking ("CCB"), Commercial & Investment Bank ("CIB") and Asset & Wealth Management ("AWM") – with the remaining activities in Corporate. The Firm's consumer business segment is CCB, and the Firm's wholesale business segments are CIB and AWM.
A description of the Firm's reportable business segments and the products and services that they provide to their respective client bases, as well as a description of Corporate activities, is provided in the Management's discussion and analysis of financial condition and results of operations section of this Form 10-K ("Management's discussion and analysis" or "MD&A") under the heading "Business Segment & Corporate Results," which begins on page 46, and in Note 32.
Competition
JPMorganChase and its subsidiaries and affiliates operate in highly competitive environments. Competitors include other banks, brokerage firms, investment banking companies, merchant banks, hedge funds, commodity trading companies, private equity firms, insurance companies, mutual fund companies, investment managers, credit card companies, mortgage banking companies, trust companies, securities processing companies, automobile financing companies, leasing companies, e-commerce and other internet-based companies, digital asset and other financial technology companies, and other companies engaged in providing similar and new products and services. The Firm's businesses generally compete on the basis of the quality and variety of the Firm's products and services, transaction execution, innovation, reputation and price. Competition also varies based on the types of clients, customers, industries and geographies served. With respect to some of its geographies and products, JPMorganChase competes globally; with respect to others, the Firm competes on a national or regional basis. New competitors in the financial services industry continue to emerge, including firms that offer products and services solely through the internet and non-financial companies that offer products and services that disintermediate traditional banking products and services offered by financial services firms such as JPMorganChase.
